description_long: This greengrocer is a vibrant, market-style building with a striking triangular roof covered in neatly arranged fresh produce, including oranges, apples, and pears, forming colorful geometric patterns. The front facade features open black-framed stalls displaying watermelons and other vegetables, while the sides and roof are lined with crates and baskets filled with various fruits and packaged goods. The structure is composed of stacked crates and bins, with the produce meticulously organized in rows and sections. The roof's apex is adorned with larger vegetables and a central display of white gourds, adding height and visual interest. The overall appearance is lively and inviting, reminiscent of a bustling street market. The building's design emphasizes accessibility and visibility of the fresh goods, with no visible doors or enclosed spaces.
description: A colorful, market-style greengrocer building with a triangular produce-covered roof and open black-framed stalls displaying fruits and vegetables. The structure is lively, accessible, and reminiscent of a street market.
description_view_0: Front view shows the geometric produce arrangement on the roof and a central circular display, with crates and bins forming the lower structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the triangular roof's height, open black-framed stalls with watermelons, and a central aisle of produce crates.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front with geometric produce patterns, but lacks the central circular display and has more visible packaged goods.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the triangular roof, open stalls with large vegetables, and a central display of white gourds at the roof's peak.
